Fix Linux test sharding to use per-project split instead of filters

Split by test project instead of complex namespace filters:
- Shard "core": runs Core.Test only (5000+ tests, the bottleneck)
- Shard "other": runs all other test assemblies in sequence
This avoids dotnet test filter length limits and ensures all tests run.

commit.message:
Speed up DatabaseTargetFixture by polling instead of sleeping

Replace Thread.Sleep(1000) with polling loop that checks for DB writes
every 50ms with a 2s timeout. Tests complete as soon as the async log
batch flushes (~500ms) instead of always waiting the full second.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

clean up render refactor: remove dead code, use typed models throughout

- Remove ~260 lines of dead legacy methods on ViewResolver (format_state,
  resolve_auto_entities, fetch_logbook_entries, fetch_weather) that were
  no longer called after SwiftBar decoupling
- ViewResolver.resolve_view() now parses YAML through ViewConfig.model_validate(),
  and all resolve methods accept typed Pydantic config models instead of raw dicts
- check_visibility() uses isinstance on typed VisibilityCondition discriminated
  union instead of dict key access
- Fix heading double-resolution in _resolve_section by caching the resolved
  RenderedHeading instead of re-resolving from raw config
- Add CachedEntityState model replacing dict[str, dict[str, Any]] state cache
- Add typed AutoEntitiesIncludeRule, AutoEntitiesNotFilter, AutoEntitiesNotCondition
  models replacing list[dict[str, Any]] filter rules
- Add typed LogbookTarget and AutoEntitiesInnerCardConfig models
- Fix pyright errors in swiftbar.py: add proper imports for HAClient and Path
- Fix EntityBadgeConfig.state_content type to accept list[str] | str | None

commit.message:
Fix annotation tools: synchronous engine setup after awaited image load

The previous fix still created the Konva engine inside img.onload — an
async callback where $state writes (canvasWidth, canvasHeight) trigger
Svelte 5 reactivity flushes that can destroy stage event handlers before
they finish registering.

Restructure loadCapture() to:
1. await loadImage() to get dimensions (also yields for {#if} DOM flush)
2. Set $state dimensions and await tick() for DOM update
3. Synchronously create engine, wire events, set background

No $state writes happen inside callbacks now — everything after the two
awaits is fully synchronous, eliminating the reactivity race condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: enable audio playback for story videos

Configure AVAudioSession with .playback category so video audio plays
through the speaker. Without this, AVPlayer defaults to silent output.
Uses .playback (not .soloAmbient) to play even with the silent switch
on, matching Instagram Stories behavior.

commit.message:
Speed up IgdbProxyRetryFixture tests from 32s to <2s

Extract virtual RetryDelayBaseMs property and EnforceRateLimit method
in IgdbProxy so tests can override them to eliminate Thread.Sleep delays
and rate limiting. Test subclass TestableIgdbProxy sets delay to 0 and
skips rate limiting entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix QZ private key permissions for bind-mounted containers

chmod 600 prevents www-data (UID 33) from reading the key when it's
bind-mounted from a host file owned by a different UID. Use 644
since the file is only accessible within the Docker host filesystem.

Phase 1: Platform foundation, real data integration, PCB + calculator

- Fix CSS: wrap custom classes in @layer components for proper Tailwind v4 cascade
- Auth: NextAuth.js with credentials provider, middleware route protection
- Landing page: Editorial hero with "Portland deserves better answers"
- Dashboard: moved to /dashboard route group with Header/Footer via layout
- Deep-dive pages: /dashboard/[question] with full detail view per metric
- Embeddable widgets: /dashboard/embed/[question] for journalist iframes
- CSV export: /api/export/[question] endpoint
- Public API: /api/public/metrics returning all 7 headlines
- Real data integration: ArcGIS helper querying Portland Maps endpoints
  (BDS_Permit, Crime MapServer grids, BPS_Graffiti, CivicApps API)
- PCB certification: /apply with 3-step application form
- Benefits calculator: /calculator with savings engine (tax rates, SDCs, network value)
- Shared UI: Button, Card, Badge, Input, Select, Tabs, Table components
- ETL: ArcGIS client, updated workers with verified endpoints, new graffiti + PBOT workers
- Login/signup pages with editorial design

commit.message:
Replace simplified reflective tower with full CPS version in kamea-rs

The kamea-rs/examples/ copy was a simplified tower that didn't use the
metacircular evaluator. Replace with the full version from root examples/
so `cargo run --release -- run examples/psi_metacircular.lisp
examples/psi_reflective_tower.lisp` runs the complete demo: CPS
meta-evaluation, reify/reflect, continuation inspection, and k-if
branch swap.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: Kamea
repository.description: Axiom-driven search for finite magmas with intrinsic representation, evaluation, and control, with machine-checked proofs in Lean 4.

commit.message:
Phase 4: Canvas interactions, PDF import, panel visuals, power tab, plan groups

- Marquee selection (right-drag=window, left-drag=crossing) with multi-delete and multi-drag
- Room drawing alignment guides, room drag/move, corner vertex editing, room color picker
- Ghost devices filtered to Tree-bus only and same floor_group
- Wire snap hover indicator for ghost devices, WireDetailPanel shows ghost device names
- Fix wire distance calculation (calibration points now in blueprint space)
- Fix daisy-chain UI validation (panel reachability via BFS, not just direct panel_id)
- Multi-page PDF import with page picker, crop tool, and polyfill for pdfjs 5.5
- Floor group system: plans share ghost devices/rooms within groups, auto-sync rooms
- Import rooms from other plans in same group
- DIN rail layout view in Panels tab and Panel Manual PDF
- Power tab: PSU distribution, Class 2 compliance, DC breaker recommendations
- Cables sidebar reads from database cable_definitions instead of hardcoded defaults
- Keyboard: Delete rooms, Escape closes palette picker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rc-shared environments for Rust interpreter, fix multi-file CLI

Replace HashMap-cloning environment model with Rc<RefCell<EnvFrame>>
linked scope chain. Environment captures (closures, let, function calls)
are now O(1) pointer copies instead of O(n) HashMap clones. This fixes
OOM when running the CPS meta-circular evaluator, which previously
created quadratic memory usage from thousands of environment copies.

Key changes:
- New Env type in lisp.rs: Rc-shared linked frames with parent chain
- defun/lambda captures are O(1) Rc clones; self-reference works via
  shared frame (no more explicit clone-and-insert trick)
- let/apply_fn create child scopes instead of cloning
- CLI accepts multiple files with shared Machine state
- 64MB thread stack for deep metacircular evaluation

The full reflective tower (CPS meta-circular evaluator + reify/reflect +
continuation chain inspection + k-if branch swap) now runs to completion
in the Rust interpreter. All 40 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(vault): return zero max-borrow when user has active loan

get-max-borrow-amount reported available borrow capacity even when
the user already had an active loan. Since the contract enforces
one-loan-per-user, the effective capacity is zero. Return u0 early
to avoid misleading frontend displays.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(vault): guard blocks-elapsed calculation against underflow

Replace raw (- block-height start-block) with a guarded subtraction
that floors at zero. While block-height should always be >= start-block
in normal flow, defensive arithmetic prevents a panic if the invariant
is violated. Matches the safe-sub pattern used in v2.

security: harden K8s deployments + Dockerfiles (02-CLUSTER-HARDENING)

Helm chart (ArgoCD managed):
- readOnlyRootFilesystem: true (all 3 deployments)
- automountServiceAccountToken: false (all 3 deployments)
- CORS: restricted to https://linksmlm.com (was *)
- Templated readOnlyRootFilesystem in chroma + ui (was hardcoded false)

K8s manifests (method1):
- imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent → Always
- chromadb image pinned to @sha256: digest

Dockerfiles:
- chromadb: pinned base image to digest
- ui: added non-root USER (UID 10001) + HEALTHCHECK

Dependencies:
- npm tar bumped to 7.5.11 (4 CVEs fixed)

repository.name: Portfolio

commit.message:
fix: resolve content consistency, gesture reliability, and list performance

- Fix wrong video/photo showing when navigating quickly: add .id(story.id)
  to force SwiftUI view recreation on story change, preventing AVPlayer reuse
- Fix content repetition across users: replace randomElement() with shuffled
  pool rotation to maximize variety before wrapping
- Fix video loading: add KVO on AVPlayer.timeControlStatus to track buffering
  state, show loading indicator and delay timer until playback begins
- Fix long press not pausing: sequence LongPressGesture with DragGesture(0)
  to keep gesture alive while finger is held, instead of discrete recognition
- Fix long press releasing advances story: suppress taps for 150ms after
  hold release to prevent finger-up from triggering onTapGesture
- Fix infinite scroll stopping on re-launch: refetch content pools when
  empty (persistence loads users but pools are memory-only)
- Fix scroll lag at list bottom: replace O(n) firstIndex scan with direct
  index pass, add in-memory Set cache for seen/liked states to eliminate
  per-story SwiftData queries (~100 queries/frame → O(1) lookups)
- Add semi-transparent overlay behind loading spinner for visibility on
  bright content

commit.message:
redesign: apply brand guidelines + strengthen conversion copy

Brand identity system:
- Swap fonts → Cormorant Garamond (display, wt 300-400) + Jost (UI, wt 300-500)
- Swap palette → Ivory/Parchment/Obsidian/Charcoal/Stone/Canopy/Ember tokens
- Replace all inline styles with full CSS class system keyed to brand tokens
- Extract hummingbird-R logomark from brand guide, add to nav + footer
- Typography scale matches brand spec: light-weight serifs, positive letter-spacing
- All hover states, borders, and radii aligned to brand (2px square corners vs rounded)

Copy improvements (conversion-focused):
- Single primary CTA throughout; secondary focus-strip removed
- Hero subtitle now states ICP criteria ($300K-$700K EBITDA, B2B, 10+ yr) for instant self-qualification
- Stats bar moved directly below hero CTA for above-fold qualifier signal
- About section reframed: opens with pre-decision founder ("not ready to sell yet")
- Named specific industries: HVAC, commercial cleaning, staffing, facilities, IT services
- Physician income angle elevated to standalone headline: "We don't need this deal to work."
- Loss-aversion contrast added: conventional PE outcome described before Reivax alternative
- Anonymized deal story added (19-yr facilities company, 14% growth, zero attrition)
- Principal bio card (Xavier Robinson) with photo placeholder
- Confidentiality upgraded from promise to mechanism (mutual NDA from first conversation)
- Form: open message textarea → optional; added EBITDA range + stage-of-process selects
- Post-submit success state names Xavier and lists exact next steps
- "What happens next" numbered sequence added to contact sidebar
- Self-quote "— Reivax Mission" removed; positioning statement stands on its own

Mobile responsive:
- Full single-column stacking at ≤960px and ≤600px
- Stats bar reflows 2-col then 1-col
- Mobile hamburger nav with slide-down menu
- All section paddings reduced on small screens

commit.message:
オンライン対戦をホスト権限モデルに移行・ゲスト側バグ修正

ホスト側で全ゲームシミュレーションを実行し、ゲスト側は入力送信+描画のみにする
ホスト権限モデルに変更。これにより攻撃・ダメージ・KO判定が正確に同期される。

主な変更:
- sendOnlineSyncをsendGuestInput/sendHostStateに置換
- applyHostState: ホストからの全状態をゲスト側に適用
- updateGuestEffects: ゲスト側のエフェクト専用更新関数
- ゲストのhandleKeyDown簡略化（入力記録のみ）
- ゲスト側KOエフェクト一式（爆発・斬撃・紙吹雪・効果音）を正しく生成
- ダメージ数字のフォーマット修正（value/scale/bold）
- charIdx/animTimer/chargeMaxTime等の同期漏れ修正
- hitFlash/screenShakeをゲスト側ローカル生成に変更

commit.message:
Fix annotation tools: replace $effect engine setup with tick()-based loadCapture()

Svelte 5's $effect cleanup fires when $state variables (canvasWidth,
canvasHeight) are written inside an async img.onload callback, cancelling
the very callback that creates the Konva engine and attaches stage event
handlers — leaving all annotation tools non-functional.

Replace the $effect + active-flag pattern with an explicit async
loadCapture() function that uses tick() to guarantee the {#if} block has
rendered the canvasContainer div before creating the AnnotationEngine.
All entry points (onMount, new-capture-ready, capture-complete,
loadFromClipboard) now call loadCapture().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: automated daily reminder cron job via Vercel Cron

Adds a GET /api/cron/reminders route that finds SENT facilityOutreaches
eligible for a reminder (based on FIRST_REMINDER_DAYS=7, BETWEEN_REMINDERS_DAYS=7,
MAX_REMINDERS=3), builds and sends reminder emails (sim or real), then
updates reminderCount/lastReminderAt and inserts an outreachEvents record.
Secured with CRON_SECRET bearer token. Scheduled daily at 12:00 UTC via vercel.json.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(staking): guard total-staked and total-stakers against underflow in unstake

The regular unstake path used raw subtraction for total-staked and
total-stakers. If accounting drifts (e.g. from emergency-unstake
race), this would panic. Use the same guarded pattern already used
in emergency-unstake.

commit.message:
Enable parallel test execution for Core.Test

- Add [assembly: Parallelizable(ParallelScope.Fixtures)] to run test
  fixtures concurrently
- Make ExceptionVerification thread-safe using AsyncLocal<T> so each
  test gets its own log collection instead of sharing a static list
- Add lock to LoggingTest.InitLogging to prevent duplicate NLog target
  registration from concurrent fixture setup
- Add lock to DbTest.CreateSqliteDatabase to prevent race condition
  when creating the cached migration database
- Mark ConfigCachingFixture and DatabaseTargetFixture as
  NonParallelizable since they rely on shared global state
